scheduled_followups.why: On 2026-05-01 bg_loop_silent (sev2) fired immediately on first deploy with loop=health_monitor, age_s=250. Investigation showed no real outage — the bug was a calibration drift: bg_heartbeat.EXPECTED_INTERVAL_S["health_monitor"]=60 vs actual asyncio.sleep(300) in health_monitor.py:101. Fix landed e820ca85. The same drift class will bite again as new loops are added or sleep cadences change. This task spawns an audit fork to catch all such drifts + recommend an AST-grep CI gate.
scheduled_followups.doc: CLAUDE.md Session 214 entry on bg_heartbeat calibration; commit e820ca85
scheduled_followups.action_prompt: Spawn a research fork: walk EXPECTED_INTERVAL_S in mcp-server/central-command/backend/bg_heartbeat.py + task_defs in mcp-server/main.py; for each entry, find the corresponding loop function and grep its inter-iteration asyncio.sleep(N); flag any drift > 50%. Also list loops in task_defs without EXPECTED_INTERVAL_S coverage. Recommend an AST-grep CI gate. Report under 600 words. Research-only — no code changes from the fork.

scheduled_followups.why: CronCreate scheduled cron e6242204 for Monday 8:43 AM local but the durable flag appears non-functional in this runtime build (response said Session-only despite durable=true). Without the cross-session pattern this entry, the digest stops the moment my session ends. Future-Claude on first invocation should: (1) run the digest fork itself, (2) re-schedule the cron with CronCreate("43 8 * * 1", recurring=true, durable=true), (3) bump THIS scheduled_followups entry to next Monday to keep the self-healing chain alive.
scheduled_followups.doc: CLAUDE.md Session 214 entry on bg_heartbeat calibration (sibling pattern); CronCreate id e6242204 (in-session) — may have died
scheduled_followups.action_prompt: Spawn a research fork: read .agent/claude-progress.json::scheduled_followups, bucket entries by days_until_due (OVERDUE / DUE_THIS_WEEK / UPCOMING / LATER), cross-check any named substrate invariants against prod via SSH. Emit a markdown digest under 800 words. AFTER digest: re-schedule the weekly cron via CronCreate AND bump this scheduled_followups entry due-date to next Monday. Self-healing chain.

scheduled_followups.task: P1: persistence-drift recurrence not escalating to L2
scheduled_followups.why: Audit 2026-05-01: 406 L1 resolutions in 7d for windows_update / defender_exclusions / rogue_scheduled_tasks but ZERO L2 decisions. CLAUDE.md mandates 3+ same-type resolutions in 4h → bypass L1 → L2 with root-cause context. The recurrence detector is not routing to the persistence runbooks (RB-WIN-PERSIST-001/002, L1-PERSIST-TASK-001, etc — all exist + enabled). Investigate _recurrence_velocity_loop + recurrence threshold config + l2_decisions trigger gates.
scheduled_followups.action_prompt: Spawn fork to: (1) read _recurrence_velocity_loop in main.py and trace its emission logic, (2) check if recurrence rows are written to recurrence_patterns or similar table, (3) inspect what triggers escalation_reason=recurrence on l2_decisions, (4) confirm whether persistence runbook rules are gated on recurrence vs immediate dispatch. Report under 800 words. Research only.

scheduled_followups.task: P3 followup: extend incidents.resolution_tier VARCHAR(10) → VARCHAR(32) cleanly
scheduled_followups.why: Round-table 2026-05-01 abandoned mig 266 ALTER COLUMN TYPE during prod outage because v_canonical_incidents (mig 258 view) AND partner_site_weekly_rollup (materialized view) both reference resolution_tier. Drop+recreate of the materialized view requires REFRESH which can take minutes on prod-sized data. Shipped mig 267 instead (shorten value: auto_recovered → recovered). Architectural cleanup remains: extending the column allows future tier names without 10-char constraint.
scheduled_followups.action_prompt: Spawn fork: write mig 268 with 3 BEGIN/COMMIT blocks: DROP v_canonical_incidents + DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW partner_site_weekly_rollup → ALTER COLUMN TYPE → recreate both views + REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW. Verify refresh time empirically. Once column is wider, ship mig 269 to RENAME value back to auto_recovered for forensic clarity.

scheduled_followups.why: BUG 2 root cause was credentials: 'same-origin' on SiteComplianceHero.tsx:37 producing 401 → null → No Data on the dashboard. Fixed inline 2026-05-01. Repo grep found 63 OTHER sites with the same pattern (AuthContext, portal browser-verify, partner audit log, integrations API, multiple SiteX components, etc.). Round-table consensus: do NOT mass-rewrite without per-site verification. Some may LEGITIMATELY want 'same-origin' (e.g. portal browser-verify intentionally uses anonymous fetches for cryptographic isolation). Sweep MUST be per-site, eyes-on.
scheduled_followups.action_prompt: For each of the 63 sites in mcp-server/central-command/frontend/src/ matching `credentials: 'same-origin'`: (1) determine if the endpoint requires auth (read the route handler in backend); (2) if YES auth required → swap to 'include'; (3) if NO auth required (e.g. browser-verify, public portal home) → add `// same-origin-allowed: <reason>` opt-out marker on the line; (4) decrement BASELINE_MAX in test_no_same_origin_credentials.py by the swap count. Sites flagged by Diana for priority monitoring: SiteSLAIndicator.tsx:102, SiteActivityTimeline.tsx:113, SiteSearchBar.tsx:107.

scheduled_followups.task: BUG 3 cleanup: migrate 10 legacy compliance_status readers to get_per_device_compliance helper
scheduled_followups.why: BUG 3 Path C round-table 2026-05-01 deprecated discovered_devices.compliance_status (mig 269). 14 callers currently read the column (4 doc references + 10 real reads in partners.py, routes.py, client_portal.py, device_sync.py legacy paths). CI gate ratchet baseline is 14; lower as each is migrated. The 10 real readers return the column to the frontend for display — migration requires changing response shapes to use the helper output.
scheduled_followups.action_prompt: For each of the 10 real readers (partners.py:1864, background_tasks.py:1184, client_portal.py:1141+4441, device_sync.py:795+1288+1338, routes.py:5241+5778): replace `d.compliance_status` SELECT with a JOIN to db_queries.get_per_device_compliance() output. Decrement BASELINE_MAX in test_compliance_status_not_read.py for each migrated reader. Once BASELINE_MAX=0, ship a final migration to DROP COLUMN discovered_devices.compliance_status.
